title: Deaths from road injuries
subtitle: Annual number of deaths from road injuries. Road injuries include deaths of drivers, passengers, pedestrians and cyclists.
caption:
Deaths from road injuries, 2023

Annual number of deaths from road injuries. Road injuries include deaths of drivers, passengers, pedestrians and cyclists.

This choropleth map displays the annual number of deaths from road injuries across the globe for the year 2023, utilizing a color scale that ranges from light beige for zero deaths to deep maroon for 100,000 deaths. The visualization reveals a distinct geographic concentration of high mortality figures in Asia, where China and India both fall into the darkest category, indicating over 100,000 deaths in each nation. Brazil and the United States also appear in the second-darkest red band, signifying death tolls between 30,000 and 100,000. In contrast, the map shows significantly lower absolute numbers across much of Europe and Oceania; countries such as Australia, Canada, and most of Western Europe sit in the lighter beige and pale orange bands, representing fewer than 3,000 deaths. Large portions of Africa and the Middle East display intermediate values, with nations like Nigeria and Egypt falling in the 3,000 to 10,000 range, while others like Sudan and the Democratic Republic of the Congo appear in the 10,000 to 30,000 bracket. Greenland and several smaller island nations are marked with hatching to indicate no data is available. The distribution highlights that the highest absolute counts of road injury fatalities are clustered in the world's most populous nations, while vast regions of the northern hemisphere and South America show moderate to low totals. The data shows that the burden of road injury deaths in absolute terms is heavily concentrated in a few specific countries with large populations.
